title: Inter-Landau-Level Pair Tunneling Rigidifies the Long-Wavelength Structure Factor of the ν=1/3 Laughlin State
abstract: A textbook chain of reasoning, originating with Girvin, MacDonald, and Platzman, predicts that Landau-level mixing (LLM) at nu=1/3 should soften the long-wavelength structure factor S(L): virtual excitations suppress V_1, the magnetoroton gap closes, and the single-mode approximation then forces S(L) to increase. We test this chain on the Haldane sphere at nu=1/3, N=6, 2Q=15. (i) Perturbatively, via first-order Schrieffer-Wolff dressing of the LLL Laughlin state, fed Lowdin-renormalised pseudopotentials: the dressed state gives Delta S(1) at kappa=10 of approximately +21%, consistent with the GMP prediction (~19% softening of V_1^eff). (ii) Non-perturbatively, via rigorous two-Landau-level (LLL+SLL) exact diagonalisation using a repaired DiagHam 2LL module that retains, for the first time on the sphere, the full inter-LL scattering channels. The response reverses sign: S(1) rigidifies monotonically from 0.118 at kappa->0 to 0.080 at kappa=10, a 32% suppression. Perturbative and non-perturbative results thus disagree in sign, with a gap of ~53 percentage points between +21% (SW) and -32% (full ED). Channel-mask ablation localises the reversal to the DD<->UU pair-tunneling channel (98% of the shift). A four-way decomposition shows the three-body coherent content overpowers the perturbative admixture by ~2.5x. Repaired code: https://github.com/Cannan-1/DiagHamOnSphereOpenSource (v2.0).
summary: This paper demonstrates that Landau-level mixing in the nu=1/3 fractional quantum Hall state leads to a suppression, rather than an enhancement, of the long-wavelength structure factor. It identifies a non-perturbative inter-Landau-level pair-tunneling mechanism as the cause, contradicting traditional perturbative predictions. This finding highlights the importance of many-body coherent effects in Landau-level mixing physics.
main_problem: The paper investigates whether Landau-level mixing (LLM) at filling factor nu=1/3 causes a 'softening' (increase) or 'rigidification' (suppression) of the long-wavelength structure factor S(L), challenging the established GMP chain of reasoning.
main_result: The study finds that LLM actually causes a 32% suppression (rigidification) of the structure factor S(1), driven by a non-perturbative inter-Landau-level pair-tunneling channel (DD <-> UU) that overpowers perturbative effects.
method: The authors use rigorous two-Landau-level (2LL) Exact Diagonalization (ED) on a Haldane sphere, supplemented by Schrieffer-Wolff perturbative dressing and channel-mask ablation to isolate specific scattering contributions.
model_or_system: The system is the nu=1/3 Laughlin fractional quantum Hall state, modeled using a two-Landau-level Hamiltonian (LLL + SLL) with Coulomb interactions on a Haldane sphere geometry.
key_observables: Unprojected guiding-centre structure factor S(L) (specifically S(1)), magnetoroton gap Delta, and the real-space pair correlator g(theta).
important_parameters: The Landau-level mixing parameter kappa (ratio of Coulomb to cyclotron energy), particle number N=6, and magnetic monopole strength 2Q=15.
main_assumptions: The study assumes a two-Landau-level truncation is sufficient to capture the essential physics of the rigidification mechanism.
figures_summary: Figures demonstrate the monotonic decrease of S(1) with kappa, the failure of the GMP product to remain invariant, and a four-way decomposition showing the dominance of the AB pair-tunneling channel.
paper_structure: The paper begins by introducing the GMP prediction discrepancy, presents the 2LL ED numerical results, performs a channel-mask ablation to identify the AB channel as the driver, provides a four-way decomposition to compare perturbative vs. non-perturbative effects, and concludes with a structural fingerprint analysis of the pair-tunneling process.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Coulomb energy per particle Ec/N vs κ at N = 6, 2Q = 15 from the rigorous 2LL ED. The horizontal dashed line is the κ →0 LLL ED reference. The energy decreases monotonically with κ as expected for a constrained variational principle in which higher Landau levels are added.

FIG. 2. Channel-mask ablation summary. (a) S(1) vs κ for all eight masks. The pure-LLL Laughlin value 0.11765 is the horizontal dashed line. Masks containing AB form one family that falls by ∼0.04 between κ = 0.01 and κ = 10; masks without AB stay within 1.5 × 10−3 of the Laughlin value. (b) Ground-state energy per particle Ec/N for the same runs.
